English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Hartmann collection on Indochina
Hartmann, Paul
4298
7.5 cubic feet. 7.5 cubic feet.
[ca. 1900-1970] Photographs, illustrations from books and magazines, and a few printed articles. The Hartmann Collection's main focus is Vietnam, but there is material on Cambodia and Laos, as well as a few items on Burma and Thailand. There are numerous scenes of all parts of Indochina, with particular attention to various important architectural sites. Historical materials emphasize the colonial period. Of particular interest are an extensive collection of portraits of Vietnamese and Cambodian monarchs from the colonial period and a wide and (generally) well-labelled selection of pictures showing the many different ethnic groups in Vietnam. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 9 ======= 9 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 4449959
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Bruce Lockhart
Date completed:
August 1987
EAD encoding:
Daniel J. Keeley, October 2002
Date modified:
Jude Corina, February 2017
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
The Hartmann Collection is a compilation of photographs, illustrations from books and magazines, and a few printed articles. The main focus of the collection is Vietnam, but there is material on Cambodia and Laos, as well as a few items on Burma and Thailand. There are numerous scenes of all parts of Indochina, with particular attention to various important architectural sites (in Hue and Angkor, for example) and to the Saigon area. Included are pictures of various stores and factories in South Vietnam during the post-1954 period. Historical materials emphasize the colonial period; there are a number of illustrations taken from books and periodicals about the colonization of Vietnam and Cambodia by the French in the 19th century. Numerous photographs of individuals can be found; of particular interest are an extensive collection of portraits of Vietnamese and Cambodian monarchs from the colonial period and a wide and (generally) well-labelled selection of pictures showing the many different ethnic groups in Vietnam. There are a considerable number of maps, especially numerous are road maps, including some produced by the Vietnamese government, and street/tourist guides to Saigon. Also, catalogs of Hartmann's collections.
SERIES LIST
Series I. History
Box 1-2
Series II. Archaeology
Box 2
Series III. Maps
Box 2
Series IV. Geography
Box 3
Series V. Architecture
Box 3-4
Series VI. Art
Box 4
Series VII. Performing Arts
Box 5
Series VIII. Religion and Rituals
Box 5
Series IX. Ethnology
Box 5
Series X. Agriculture
Box 6
Series XI. Industry
Box 6
Series XII. Commerce
Box 6
Series XIII. Transportation
Box 6
Series XIV. Education
Box 6
Series XV. Miscellaneous
Box 7-8
Cite As:
Hartmann collection on Indochina, #4298, Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Maps. France -- Colonies -- Asia. Cambodia -- Maps. Vietnam -- Maps. Vietnam -- History -- 20th century. Vietnam -- History -- 19th century. Thailand -- Description and travel. Burma -- Description and travel. Laos -- Description and travel. Cambodia -- Description and travel. Vietnam -- Description and travel. Monarchy -- Cambodia. Monarchy -- Vietnam. Minorities -- Vietnam. Ethnology -- Indochina. Ethnology -- Vietnam. Cham (Southeast Asian people) Caodaism. Art, Vietnamese. Art -- Cambodia. Art, Southeast Asian. Architecture -- Cambodia. Architecture -- Vietnam. Architecture -- Asia, Southeastern. Agriculture -- Vietnam.
